title: Mafia Island Marine Park (Zoning Scheme) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Mafia Island Marine Park (Zoning Scheme) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Mafia Island Marine Park (Zoning Scheme) Regulations, 2006 and shall be deemed to have come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Marine Parks and Reserves Act, 1994 ; “ General Management Plan ” means the General Management Plan for Mafia Island adopted under section 14 of the Act ; “ core zone ” means an area within the marine park designated as a core zone as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations; “ specified use zone ” means an area within the marine park designated and shown as such in the Second Schedule to these Regulations; and “general use zone” means an area within the marine park which is not in the core zone or specified use zone. [Cap. 146]

provisions.title: Establishment of MIMP zones
provisions.content: (1) As from the first day of September 2000, the whole area comprising the Mafia Island Marine Park shall be deemed to be divided into core zones, specified use zones and general use zones in accordance with the provisions of these Regulations. (2) The areas comprising the core zones shall be as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (3) The areas comprising the specified use zones shall be as set out in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (4) All the areas within a Mafia Island Marine Park, which are not in the core zones or the specified use zones, shall be deemed to be in the general use zones, subject to the provisions of regulation. (5) Any person living or working in any zone under this regulation shall at all time, have due regard to the permitted and prohibited activities therein, as set out in the Third Schedule to these Regulations. (6) The Minister shall approve every zoning plan and amendment to a zoning plan drawn by the Board; and he shall, by notice published in the Gazette , notify the public about every such plan or amendment thereof.

provisions.title: Criteria for zoning schemes
provisions.content: In drawing a plan for zones or for a modification of zones stipulated zoning General Management Plan , the Board shall have regard to the schemes criteria for selection and resource use strategies set out in the relevant part of the General Management Plan .

provisions.title: Identification of zones
provisions.content: The Minister shall, for the purposes of demarcating the boundaries of zones established under regulation 3 , cause appropriate and visible identification marks of such material, shape and colour as he may specify, to be placed and maintained.

provisions.title: Buffer zone
provisions.content: (1) Without prejudice to the provisions of sub regulations (5) of Buffer regulation 3 , the Minister may create and determine the boundaries of a buffer zone in the area beyond the outer boundaries of the marine park subject to sub regulation (2) . (2) In the exercise of his powers under this regulation, the Minister shall consult the Mafia local government authority and the village councils of the villages affected. (3) The Minister shall on creation and modification of the boundaries of the buffer zone, by notice published in the Gazette , notify the public. (4) Until notified otherwise pursuant to this regulation, the buffer zone for the Mafia Island Marine Park shall be the whole area of a width of eight hundred metres outward from the present boundary of the Mafia Island Marine Park as shown in the existing General Management Plan .

provisions.title: Stakeholders
provisions.content: In the exercise of his power to determine or modify the areas of stakethe zones within the Mafia Island Marine Park and the buffer zone, the holders Minister shall cause adequate consultations to be made with the village councils in the area, the Mafia local government authority, fishermen and other residents. First Schedule (Made under Regulation 3(2)) Areas designated as Core Zones within Mafia Island Marine Park 1. Outer Kinasi pass/Mchangani Core Zone boundary The northern terrestrial boundary is the mean high water mark; bounded in the Outer West by a line running from the West side of the Mchangani mangroves to the North end Kinasi of Miewe Kubwa, from the Southern tip of Miewe Kubwa to the northern tip of Juani Pae Island; bounded in the East by a line running one kilometer parallel to the mouth of Mchangani Kinasi Pass, the line of the outer reef. 2. Kitulia Core Zone boundary This encloses a sand cay surrounded by a circular reef, the boundary is a line one Core Zone kilometer outward from the mean low-water mark. 3. Kijiwenyara Core Zone boundary (1) Kijiwenyara is a small rocky islet situated off the western end of Bwejuu Core Zone Island. (2) The zone covers the islet itself bounded by the mean low-water mark, and marine area described by a semi-circle of radius two hundred meters with the center of the circle at the western most end of the islet. 4. Mlola forest Core Zone boundary The main part of Mlola Forest is lying within the marine park running from the Forest northern boundary of the park in Jimbo village, south along the eastern seaboard to Core Zone Mchangani; Bounded in the east by the mean low water mark, in the west by the Mlola Boundary Forest Specified use Zone and the official forest boundary. 5. Kua Ruins Core Zone boundary Is a continuous area, surrounding all the Kua ruins, on Juani Island extending one Core Zone hundred metres in all directions from the outer ruins. 6. Kizani Channel Core Zone boundary The water channel at the south-western comer of Juani Island known locally as Mto wa Kizani, extending up to the high-water mark in the channel itself, plus the beach areas and Core Zone shallow waters on either side of the east end of the channe. 7. Ras Kisimani Ruins core boundary Small enclaves containing the ruins themselves and their immediate vicinity. Second Schedule (Made under Regulation 3(3)) Areas designated as specified - use Zones 1. Chole bay specified use Zone boundary An extensive section of the north eastern and central part of Chole Bay, encompassing Kolawe Reef to the west and Msubiji Reef to the south; bounded from a point on the northern shore of Chole Bay east of Mchangani, running south west towards Chole Island to a point south-west of Kulawe reef, running east to the northern tip of Juani on the east side the boundary is adjacent to the boundary of the Outer Kinasi Pass Core Zone. 2. Kifinge Bay specified use Zone boundary A coastal or marine strip running the full length of Kifinge Bay; bounded in the west, by means of low-water mark, adjacent to the Mlola Forest Core Zone in the; and in the east by a line one kilometre from, and parallel to, the low-tide mark. 3. Juani outer reef specified use Zone boundary A coastal or marine strip runing the full length of the eastern seaboard of Juani Island including the islet known as la Kasa, at the southern end of Juani; bounded inland by a line of fifty metres above the mean high-water mark, and in the east, by a line of one kilometre from, and parallel to, the mean low-water mark. Specified- 4. Jibondo outer reef specified use Zone boundary A coastal or marine strip runing the full length of the eastern seaboard of Jibondo Island; bounded inland by a line of fifty meters above the mean high-water mark; and in the east, by a line of one kilometre from, and parallel to, the mean low-water mark. 5. Managereef Specified use Zone Boundary A circular reef exposed at low spring tides; the zone boundary extended one kilometre out from the mean low-water mark. 6. Mlola Forest Specified use Zone Boundary Two narrow strips runing two hundred and fifty metres along the inside of the official forest boundary of the Mlola Forest Reserve; one runs, from Beacon 90 south of Kitotoni River (Beacon 54); and the other, runs from the Mlola Forest Post (Beacon 43) in Kungwi south to Chole Bay (Beacon 1). 7. Ras Kisimani Specified use Zone Boundary All of the land area on Ras Kisimani above the low-spring tide mark, plus the two Ras channels that partially separate it from the main Mafia Island; Mto wa Arusha and Mto Kisimani wa Saliboko, up to the high tide mark on the east side of the channels; this includes the extensive mangrove stretch in the inter-tidal area, north of Ras Kisimani up to a latitudinal line above the northern mouth of Mto wa Arusha, such that all of the mangroves around the mouth of the channel and to the south are included. the General Management Plan for Mafia Island adopted under section 14 of the Act
an area within the marine park designated as a core zone as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations
an area within the marine park designated and shown as such in the Second Schedule to these Regulations
an area within the marine park which is not in the core zone or specified use zone. [Cap. 146]
